threads.content:
Marketing agencies should be terrified of their own clients.

Not because clients are getting smarter. Because clients now have AI tools that can do 80% of what agencies sell. Website builds, landing pages, email templates, social graphics. All generated in minutes for pennies.

The $50K website project is collapsing. Clients know it. Agencies know it. Nobody wants to say it out loud.

Are you selling production or strategy?

What happens to the 2 million web developers who build marketing sites?

AI website builders can now generate a complete, styled, responsive marketing website from a single prompt. Sitemaps, wireframes, style guides, and production-ready code. In minutes.

Where does your current work sit on the complexity spectrum?

instagram.caption:
AI website builders crossed the production-quality threshold. 10x generates sitemaps, wireframes, style guides, and full pages from one prompt. Day 14 of 30.

#ai #artificialintelligence #tech #developer #startup #coding #automation #futureofwork

Save this for later. Follow @founderswing for daily AI breakdowns.

facebook.caption:
AI website builders crossed the production-quality threshold. 10x generates sitemaps, wireframes, style guides, and full pages from one prompt. Day 14 of 30.

What do you think? Drop your take below.

tweets: Marketing agencies should be terrified of their own clients.

Not because clients are getting smarter. Because clients now have AI tools that can do 80% of what agencies sell. Website builds, landing pages, email templates, social graphics. All generated in minutes for pennies.
The $50K website project is collapsing. Clients know it. Agencies know it. Nobody wants to say it out loud.

The agencies that will survive are repositioning right now. They're selling strategy, not production. They're selling audience understanding, market positioning, brand narrative. The things AI can generate a draft for but can't originate.
Production skills are depreciating at roughly 40% per year. That's not a guess, it's what the pricing data shows. What was worth $5K last year now costs $500 or is free.
The smartest marketers I know are building AI-first workflows and charging for decisions, not deliverables. "I'll tell you exactly what to build and why" is worth more than "I'll build it for you."

The question isn't whether AI will eat marketing production. It already has. The question is whether you're selling the thing AI can't do.
Are you selling production or strategy?

twitter.content: AI website builders crossed the production-quality threshold
